title: Add OnBuild support for Dockerfiles
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

This is a WIP to hopefully allow @nalind to spot what I'm messing up with this.  The image is not getting the OnBuild value attached to it, and I'm not completely sure why.  Hoping Nalin can quickly spot my mistake and give me a resounding head slap.

comments.body: Wow, this turns out to be a little more complicated than I first expected.  You're most of the way there, though!
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 1ad8dc0) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

Test Results: 
```
# buildah bud -f ~/Dockerfile -t onbuild-image 
STEP 1: FROM fedora:28
STEP 2: RUN touch bar
STEP 3: ONBUILD RUN touch bar
STEP 4: RUN touch /foo
STEP 5: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.override_kernel_check=true]localhost/onbuild-image:latest
Getting image source signatures

42cda3f6e105725f507146e1b7ce8a7aad41971567316086149eaea6f9628dba

STEP 1: FROM onbuild-image
STEP 2: RUN touch /baz
STEP 3: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.override_kernel_check=true]localhost/result-image:latest
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:891e1e4ef82ad02a4ea1f030831f942d722c7694c4db64ca3239c8163b811c58

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
05b73d6d687068c4f9a5894717583a8e2244568f46e0adfec1bd4b4539c027b9

# ctr=$(buildah from result-image)

# buildah run $ctr ls /bar
/bar
```

@TomSweeneyRedHat 

In this execution
`# buildah bud -f ~/Dockerfile-2 -t result-image .`

Shouldn't the output show the user that it is firing the `ONBUILD RUN` as the first RUN command?

eg.
```
STEP 1: FROM onbuild-image
STEP 2: RUN touch bar
STEP 3: RUN touch /baz
```

I will check with Docker build to confirm behaviour.

-edit-

Adding to this.. isn't the test adding 'touch bar' to the original image?
```
# buildah bud -f ~/Dockerfile -t onbuild-image 
STEP 1: FROM fedora:28
STEP 2: RUN touch bar
```

So it's not confirming the `ONBUILD RUN` ran on the second container build (because `bar` exists in the original container?)
@pixdrift That is my understanding.  I would want to to make sure that the commands run on the second buildah bud or docker build from the container image.

I''ll have to play around with Docker to see what exactly is going on there.  As an FYI, the ONBUILD command is creating the `/bar` directory in the first (onbuild-image) container.  Just for the record, that creation was not in the Dockerfile via a RUN command.  I think the end result is correct, just the road travelled may be off, but TBD on that.  Here are the Dockerfiles for reference:

```
# cat Dockerfile
FROM fedora:28
RUN touch /foo
ONBUILD RUN touch /bar

# cat Dockerfile-2
FROM onbuild-image
RUN touch /baz
```

Docker run.  Note the `# Executing 1 build trigger...` line in the first build.  The /bar directory is only present in the second image, not the first.

```
# docker build  -t onbuild-image .
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.72 GB
Step 1/3 : FROM fedora:28
Trying to pull repository docker.io/library/fedora ... 
sha256:7ae08e5637170eb47c01e315b6e64e0d48c6200d2942c695d0bee61b38c65b39: Pulling from docker.io/library/fedora
e71c36a80ba9: Pull complete 
Digest: sha256:7ae08e5637170eb47c01e315b6e64e0d48c6200d2942c695d0bee61b38c65b39
Status: Downloaded newer image for docker.io/fedora:28
 ---> cc510acfcd70
Step 2/3 : RUN touch /foo
 ---> Running in a8a45d58d3a7
 ---> 9d475a707a1d
Removing intermediate container a8a45d58d3a7
Step 3/3 : ONBUILD run touch /bar
 ---> Running in 0c8f9fbaea11
 ---> 5a47742d391a
Removing intermediate container 0c8f9fbaea11
Successfully built 5a47742d391a

# docker build -f Dockerfile-2 -t result-image .
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.72 GB
Step 1/2 : FROM onbuild-image
# Executing 1 build trigger...
Step 1/1 : RUN touch /bar
 ---> Running in 6b7f21a96a7e
 ---> c179eb6e01ee
Removing intermediate container 6b7f21a96a7e
Step 2/2 : RUN touch /baz
 ---> Running in 0aec140ff8ce
 ---> 301e60c5f24a
Removing intermediate container 0aec140ff8ce
Successfully built 301e60c5f24a

# docker run result-image ls /bar
/bar

# docker run onbuild-image ls /bar
ls: cannot access '/bar': No such file or directory
```
@TomSweeneyRedHat And how does buildah bud compare?

Had a thought on my way home and unhappily confirmed it.  The bar file is getting created in the first container, not in the second container/image vi the OnBuild command.  Because the file itself got copied during the second image build from the first image, it looked like OnBuild worked.  In reality if the OnBuild command had been "date >> /tmp/date.txt" the value would be the original date, not an updated one.  Crud.  I've  a suspicion that something in the imagebuilder code isn't quite right.

@rhatdan.  What's happening with this current code is the OnBuild is set for the first image before it goes into the imagebuilder code and when it's in the imagebuilder code it gets executed.  So in the example, the ONBUILD touch /bar happens at the first image creation time rather than when the second image is created from it.

So I've been trying to figure out how to get the OnBuild value of the first image to be set and stored after it goes through the processing of imagebuilder.  I thought I could go somewhere in image.go to do this and in the outdated comments you can see that I tried adding near line 160

```
dimage.Config.OnBuild = i.onBuild
```

So the value that I've captured in the onBuildCmd variable in imagebuildah/build.go has the value in it that we need.  If you can figure out where I can push that too, I think we'll be golden.  I've had a bear of a time working my way through all of the intertwined variable and structures representing the image in different stages/code.
